mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in real short-term interest rates and stable nominal policy rates signals improving real liquidity conditions, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of positive industrial production growth, expanding manufacturing output signals strengthening economic momentum that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the predictive power vanishes when conditioning on recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months with payroll growth, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a collapsing housing market and reduced construction activity, which drags down related sectors and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances following a greater than 5% housing starts decline, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real policy rates turn negative and signal potential future aggressive tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The inflation-minus-rates spread fails to predict negative returns, or forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-spread observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during economic expansions signals robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP acceleration coincides with negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the predictive relationship reverses during expansion regimes.
